zotero.abstractNote: We propose a framework for kernel-based two-sample and independence testing for non-stationary random processes. By leveraging the theory of reproducing kernel Hilbert spaces (RKHS) of functions on time-series, we extend existing kernel tests to the non-stationary setting. We show that our tests are consistent and provide a theoretical analysis of their power. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on synthetic and real-world data, including climate and financial time series.
zotero.conferenceName: 7th International Conference on Time Series and Forecasting (ITISE 2021)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: 7th International Conference on Time Series and Forecasting (ITISE 2021)
